title: Yoneda
text:
{transport}({#1,#2})


Definition 1 A natural transformation between two type families \(F,G:A\rightarrow\mathcal{U}\) is a family of maps \[
    \alpha:(x:A)\rightarrow(F(x)\rightarrow G(x))
\]
We denote the type of natural transformations between \(F\) and \(G\) by \(\mathrm{Nat}(F,G)\).


Definition 2 The Yoneda embedding \(よ_A:A\rightarrow(A\rightarrow\mathcal{U})\) is defined by \[
    よ_A(a):\equiv \lambda x.(a=x).
\]


Theorem 1 For any type family \(F:A\rightarrow\mathcal{U}\), the map \[
\begin{align*}
    f:\mathrm{Nat}(よ_A(a),F)&\rightarrow F(a)\\
    \alpha&\mapsto\alpha(a)(\mathrm{refl}_a)
\end{align*}
\]
is an equivalence.


Proof. We construct the inverse map \[
\begin{align*}
    g:F(a)&\rightarrow\mathrm{Nat}(よ_A(a),F)\\
    u&\mapsto J(\lambda)
\end{align*}
\]
